lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Welcome adventurer, to the world renowned Wizards' Tower, home to the Order of Wizards. How may I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Well, take care adventurer. You stand on the ruins of the old destroyed Wizards' Tower. Strange and powerful magicks lurk here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teleport me to the Rune Essence Mine?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Seventior disthine molenko!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have news on Persten.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but she's gone.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I see. It was always unlikely that she'd survive the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't the Abyss. I think she might have been dead long before then. The Persten we spoke to was an imposter.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: An imposter? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Mahjarrat called Sliske was disguised as her.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I can't believe it. Persten and I had our differences, but this...

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'd better share this news with the others. Thank you for bringing it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things at the Temple of the Eye?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Between the Guardians of the Rift and ourselves, we are managing to keep the Abyss at bay.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: However, despite all our research, we are no closer to working out how to close the rift permanently.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Gaining a better understanding of the artefact that was once there is key, but we have found little mention of this 'Eye'.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'm sure the wizards of the first tower had plenty of research on it. After all, they knew enough about it to use its symbol as the symbol for our order.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: All that knowledge lost because of those accursed Zamorakians!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know if it will be much help, but I found out its real name. It's called the Stone of Jas.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hmm... That could be useful. I'll have a dig around and see if we can find any mention of it.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Anyway, if you wish to help defend the temple, just head through the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were you able to find any information on the Stone of Jas?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Not yet. So far we've still only been able to find a few small mentions of it, but they all call it the Eye.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'm sure the wizards of the first tower had plenty of research that could have helped. All that knowledge lost because of those accursed Zamorakians!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Anyway, if you wish to help defend the temple, just head through the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might do that, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who else knows the teleport to the Rune Essence Mine?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Apart from myself, there's also Aubury in Varrock, Wizard Cromperty in East Ardougne, Brimstail in the Tree Gnome Stronghold and Wizard Distentor in Yanille's Wizards' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: My pleasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me about the old Wizards' Tower?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Of course. The first Wizards' Tower was built at the same time the Order of Wizards was founded. It was at the dawn of the Fifth Age, when the secrets of runecrafting were rediscovered.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: For years, the tower was a hub of magical research. Wizards of all races and religions were welcomed into our order.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Alas, that openness is what ultimately led to disaster. The wizards who served Zamorak, the evil god of chaos, tried to claim our magical discoveries in his name.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: They failed, but in retaliation, they burnt the entire tower to the ground. Years of work was destroyed.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: The tower was soon rebuilt of course, but even now we are still trying to regain knowledge that was lost.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: That's why I spend my time down here, in fact. This basement is all that is left of the old tower, and I believe there are still some secrets to discover here.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Of course, one secret I am no longer looking for is the teleportation incantation to the Rune Essence Mine. We have you to thank for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard a different take on how the tower burnt down. Something about a ritual gone wrong?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Nothing more than Zamorakian hearsay! They burnt the tower down and are now too cowardly to even admit it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks, I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Well, take care. You stand on the ruins of the old destroyed Wizards' Tower. Strange and powerful magicks lurk here.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Well done.
